metadata.description: Sub-national influence entities and their relationships to UN voting behavior

entities.name: NATO
entities.influence: Coordinates opposition to nuclear disarmament resolutions. Members vote as bloc on security issues.

entities.name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation
entities.influence: Coordinates resistance to Western-drafted human rights resolutions. Growing counterweight to NATO.

entities.name: African Union
entities.influence: Common position mechanism: 54 states coordinate before GA votes. Strongest cohesion on decolonization, Palestine, development.

entities.name: Organisation of Islamic Cooperation
entities.influence: 57 members. Near-unanimous on Palestine resolutions. Split on sectarian issues (Saudi vs Iran).

entities.name: ASEAN
entities.influence: Non-interference doctrine. Often abstains collectively on sovereignty-sensitive resolutions. South China Sea disputes create internal tension.

entities.name: BRICS+
entities.influence: Expanded 2024. Coordinates on development finance, UN reform, and multipolar world order. New Development Bank as alternative to World Bank.

entities.name: Quad (Indo-Pacific)
entities.influence: Informal. Aligns India closer to Western positions on security resolutions. Counterbalances China in Indo-Pacific.

entities.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
entities.influence: Coordinates on economic resolutions. Oil wealth creates aid dependency in smaller nations. Split during Qatar crisis (2017-2021).

entities.name: Saudi Aramco / OPEC+ influence
entities.influence: Oil-producing states coordinate to weaken climate resolutions. Aramco lobbying reduces Paris Agreement enforcement language.

entities.name: US Defense Industry (Lockheed, Raytheon, Boeing)
entities.influence: Arms sales create alignment. Countries buying US weapons rarely vote against US on security resolutions. $200B+ in active contracts.

entities.name: Russian Arms Exports (Rosoboronexport)
entities.influence: India is largest buyer. Arms dependency correlates with abstention (not Yes, not No) on Russia-critical resolutions.

entities.name: Belt & Road Initiative / Huawei 5G
entities.influence: Infrastructure debt creates voting obligation. BRI recipient countries vote with China 15% more than non-recipients on sovereignty resolutions.

entities.name: US Foreign Aid (USAID)
entities.influence: Top recipients rarely vote against US. Egypt moderates on Palestine in exchange for $1.3B annual aid. Jordan receives $1.5B.

entities.name: Chinese Development Finance
entities.influence: AidData estimates $843B in Chinese development finance (2000-2021). Recipients shift 8-12% toward China on sovereignty votes.

entities.name: EU Single Market Access
entities.influence: EU candidate/associate countries align voting to maintain trade access and accession prospects.

entities.name: China as #1 Trade Partner
entities.influence: China is top trade partner for 130+ countries. Trade volume correlates with softer language on Xinjiang/Taiwan resolutions.

entities.name: Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty
entities.influence: P5 committed to disarmament under Article VI but consistently vote against disarmament resolutions. Non-parties (India, Pakistan, Israel) abstain.

entities.name: ICC Rome Statute
entities.influence: Non-parties (US, Russia, China, India, Israel) oppose ICC-referral resolutions. Parties support international criminal justice mechanisms.

entities.name: Paris Climate Agreement
entities.influence: Universal participation but enforcement contentious. AOSIS pushes binding targets, fossil fuel states (Saudi, Russia) resist enforcement language.

influence_edges.mechanism: Nuclear deterrence doctrine. NATO nuclear-sharing means 30+ countries vote No on disarmament.
Members resist country-specific human rights resolutions, citing sovereignty and non-interference.
African Union common position on decolonization, Palestine, and African development. Near-unanimous Yes votes.
57 OIC members vote Yes on Palestine resolutions with >98% cohesion. Strongest single-issue bloc.
OPEC+ members negotiate to remove binding emissions targets from economic development resolutions.
Arms purchasers vote with US on arms control to maintain supply relationship. Correlation: 73%.
Russian arms buyers (India, Egypt, Algeria) abstain rather than oppose Russia on security votes.
BRI recipients abstain on Xinjiang/Hong Kong resolutions. Won't support, won't oppose. Debt leverage.
Egypt, Jordan receive billions → moderate on Palestine (still vote Yes but softer language). Israel votes No.
Recipients shield China from human rights criticism. Voting shift measured at 8-12% on sovereignty issues.
EU accession candidates align with EU common foreign policy to demonstrate readiness.
Trade-dependent countries avoid co-sponsoring anti-China resolutions. Abstain rate increases.
BRICS+ coordinates on right-to-development, South-South cooperation, and UN reform resolutions.
Pulls India toward Western security positions but India maintains non-aligned stance on most votes.
